top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: Peace dominates the evening: Pakistan, mediating between Washington and Tehran, says the US and Iran are expected to sign an initial deal within 24 hours to end their war, with Iran signalling the Strait of Hormuz would reopen — even as US forces struck Iranian drones near the strait and Iran's state TV set funeral dates for late Supreme Leader Khamenei. In the US, Trump said a military strike killed a Venezuelan gang leader, a Warner Bros-Paramount mega-merger was cleared, and Elon Musk became the world's first trillionaire. Anthropic disabled its most advanced AI models after a US order barring foreign access.

stories.headline: Pakistan says US and Iran expected to sign initial deal within 24 hours
stories.summary: Mediator Pakistan said Washington and Tehran are expected to sign an initial agreement within 24 hours to wind down their war, with Iran signalling the Strait of Hormuz would reopen under a deal. A US official earlier cautioned the parties were 'not quite at the finish line.' The optimism came even as new military action flared near the strait; terms remain contested.

stories.headline: Iran's state TV sets funeral dates for late Supreme Leader Khamenei
stories.summary: Iran's state TV said the funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei will begin on July 4, with burial set for July 9. The announcement, attributed to Iranian state media, comes as Tehran and Washington edge toward a deal to end their conflict.

stories.headline: Trump says US military strike killed leader of Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua
stories.summary: President Trump said a US-Venezuela operation killed the leader of the prison gang Tren de Aragua. The claim, attributed to Trump, came amid a wider crackdown; reporting described Venezuelan mining towns left deserted after the army operation. Independent confirmation of the strike's outcome was not immediately available.

stories.headline: Anthropic disables most advanced AI models after US order limiting foreign access
stories.summary: Anthropic said it would abruptly disable its most advanced AI models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the US government ordered it to suspend access for foreign nationals — whether inside or outside the US — citing national-security concerns. The move affects users globally while the company complies with the order.

stories.headline: US Justice Department clears Paramount's $111bn acquisition of Warner Bros
stories.summary: The US Justice Department approved Paramount's roughly US$111 billion acquisition of Warner Bros, clearing one of the largest media mergers in years and reshaping the Hollywood landscape.

stories.headline: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX soars in stock-market debut
stories.summary: Elon Musk became the world's first trillionaire on paper after SpaceX surged in its long-awaited stock-market debut, lifting his net worth past the milestone. Wall Street climbed on the listing even as oil slid on Middle East peace hopes.

stories.headline: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center in predawn operation
stories.summary: President Trump's name was removed from the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts in a predawn operation, after a judge rejected a bid to halt the change. Litigation over the renaming continues.

stories.headline: China 'strongly dissatisfied' with Pentagon move against top Chinese tech firms
stories.summary: Beijing said it was 'strongly dissatisfied' after the Pentagon moved against several leading Chinese technology companies, the latest flashpoint in US-China tech and security tensions. China vowed to protect the interests of its firms.

stories.headline: Switzerland to vote on plan to cap its population at 10 million
stories.summary: Switzerland will hold a referendum on a proposal to cap its population at 10 million, a plan driven by debate over immigration and resources. The vote pits growth and labour needs against concerns about housing and infrastructure.

stories.headline: Thailand mourns Princess Bajrakitiyabha, who has died aged 47
stories.summary: Mourners gathered at Thailand's royal palace to await the body of Princess Bajrakitiyabha, the eldest daughter of King Vajiralongkorn, who died aged 47 after a long illness. Singapore's government conveyed its condolences.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: A mix of policy, community and safety news this evening. Senior Minister of State Sim Ann said Singapore takes a targeted, national-interest approach to online regulation, while Pasir Ris-Changi residents will get discounted food and services under a new programme. Six people were taken to hospital and 120 evacuated after a Sengkang HDB fire, port operator PSA is appealing a guilty verdict over a 2017 worker death, and the Republic conveyed condolences over the death of a Thai princess. Expect thundery showers, 26-34°C.

stories.headline: Singapore takes targeted approach to online regulation, focuses on national interest: Sim Ann
stories.summary: Senior Minister of State Sim Ann said Singapore takes a targeted approach to regulating online content, prioritising the national interest rather than broad controls, in remarks addressing how the country handles harmful and divisive material online.

stories.headline: Pasir Ris-Changi residents to get discounted food and services under new programme
stories.summary: Residents in Pasir Ris-Changi will be able to access discounted food and services under a new community programme aimed at easing cost-of-living pressures, organisers said.

stories.headline: Six taken to hospital, 120 evacuated after fire at Sengkang HDB flat
stories.summary: A fire at a Rivervale Street HDB flat in Sengkang sent six people to hospital and forced about 120 residents to evacuate, the SCDF said. The blaze was extinguished and the cause is being investigated.

stories.headline: PSA pays fine over 2017 Keppel Terminal worker death, but appeals verdict
stories.summary: Port operator PSA has paid a fine over a worker's death at Keppel Terminal in 2017 but is appealing the guilty verdict and sentence, court proceedings showed.

stories.headline: Police hunt driver after car skids onto pavement in Sengkang
stories.summary: Police are searching for a driver after a car skidded onto a pavement in Sengkang. Investigations are ongoing.

stories.headline: Singapore deeply saddened by death of Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha
stories.summary: The Singapore government said it was deeply saddened by the death of Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol, conveying condolences to the Thai royal family and people.

stories.headline: How arts schools in Singapore are adapting to AI and grading creativity
stories.summary: Singapore's arts schools are rethinking how they teach and assess creativity as generative AI tools spread, weighing how to grade originality and use the technology without dulling students' own craft.

stories.headline: Orange Yakult fans mourn as classic flavour is discontinued
stories.summary: Singaporeans are bidding a nostalgic farewell to Orange Yakult, with fans of the cultured-milk drink mourning online as the flavour is discontinued and replaced by a peach version.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The US-Iran war looks closest to ending: mediator Pakistan says an initial deal could be signed within 24 hours, Iran signals the Strait of Hormuz would reopen, and Iranian state TV has set funeral dates for late Supreme Leader Khamenei — though US forces still struck Iranian drones near the strait. In the Levant, Israel carried out air strikes in south Lebanon after evacuation warnings and Hezbollah says it confronted advancing troops. Russia-Ukraine grinds on with cross-border drone strikes and a Swedish interception of Russian jets.

theatres.name: US/Israel–Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Peace talks at the brink — mediator says an initial US-Iran deal may be signed within 24 hours and Hormuz could reopen, but US strikes on Iranian drones near the strait continued.

timeline.event: Satellite images indicate more than 50 Iranian military bases were damaged in US strikes since the war began.
US forces strike Iranian drones near the Strait of Hormuz while talks continue for an interim deal.
The UAE is reportedly set to unlock billions of dollars for Iran, according to sources, as part of moves to support a deal.
Mediator Pakistan says the US and Iran are expected to sign an initial deal within 24 hours; Iran signals the Strait of Hormuz would reopen.
Iran's state TV says the funeral for late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei will begin July 4, with burial on July 9.

theatres.name: Israel–Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Israel carried out air strikes in south Lebanon after issuing evacuation warnings; Hezbollah says it confronted advancing Israeli troops.

timeline.event: Hezbollah says it confronted Israeli troops advancing in south Lebanon.
Lebanon reports Israeli air strikes in the south after an Israeli evacuation warning.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine
theatres.status: Cross-border drone strikes continue; Sweden scrambled fighters to intercept Russian jets near its border.

timeline.event: Ukraine hikes military pay and seeks more foreign fighters, President Zelensky says.
A Ukrainian drone strike kills one person and sparks a fire at the Russian port of Temryuk, the regional governor says (claim not independently verified).
Swedish fighter jets intercept Russian aircraft near the border, Sweden's military says.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: The standout threat remains the Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda. In its latest Disease Outbreak News (13 June), the WHO reports 695 confirmed cases and 138 deaths across the two countries, with cases rising rapidly in DRC and spreading into new health zones — and Uganda has slammed 'unfair' Ebola-related air-travel restrictions. The cruise-ship Andes hantavirus cluster (13 cases, 3 deaths) has had no WHO update since late May and appears contained. Health workers and travellers in affected regions should follow official guidance.

outbreaks.name: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) — DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.status: Cases rising rapidly in DRC and spreading into new health zones (WHO); risk in DRC assessed as very high.
outbreaks.scope_note: Confirmed cases/deaths across DRC + Uganda combined per WHO Disease Outbreak News (DON607, 13 Jun 2026): 676 confirmed/136 deaths in DRC (as of 10 Jun, CFR 20.1%) and 19 confirmed/2 deaths in Uganda (as of 11 Jun); at least 37 recovered. Bundibugyo (Ebola) strain; outbreak declared mid-May 2026.

outbreaks.name: Hantavirus (Andes virus) — M/V Hondius cruise cluster
outbreaks.status: No WHO update since 28 May; ship cluster appears contained, figures now stale.
outbreaks.scope_note: 11 confirmed + 2 probable cases and 3 deaths as of 27 May 2026 (WHO DON, 28 May), among passengers/crew of the Netherlands-flagged M/V Hondius spread across several countries. Limited human-to-human spread (Andes virus); no new WHO update in over two weeks, so figures may be outdated.

stories.headline: WHO: Ebola spreading into new areas of northeastern DR Congo as cases climb
stories.summary: The WHO said the Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak is spreading into new areas of northeastern DR Congo, with confirmed cases and deaths rising rapidly. Its latest Disease Outbreak News (13 June) puts the combined DRC and Uganda toll at 695 confirmed cases and 138 deaths, with DRC cases reported across 29 health zones and isolation capacity lagging need.

stories.headline: Uganda slams 'unfair' Ebola air-travel restrictions
stories.summary: Uganda criticised what it called 'unfair' air-travel restrictions imposed over the Ebola outbreak, arguing the measures penalise the country despite its containment efforts as the linked DRC outbreak grows.

stories.headline: Kenya: mother finds body of son two days after Ebola quarantine-centre protests
stories.summary: A mother found the body of her missing son two days after protests at an Ebola quarantine centre in Kenya, underscoring tensions around regional containment measures as the DR Congo outbreak spreads.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Supply-chain compromises and actively-exploited flaws lead the cycle. Attackers hijacked 400+ Arch Linux AUR packages to push an infostealer and eBPF rootkit, and researchers found China-linked actors had backdoored Linux login software for nearly a decade. Two critical exploited bugs are on CISA's must-patch list — a maximum-severity Ivanti Sentry flaw (CVE-2026-10520, CVSS 10) and an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day (CVE-2026-35273, CVSS 9.8) used by ShinyHunters. AI-agent attack surface is heating up (Agentjacking, LangGraph RCE, OpenClaw), several large breaches landed, and the US ordered Anthropic to cut foreign access to its top models.

stories.headline: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR packages hijacked to deploy infostealer and eBPF rootkit
stories.summary: More than 400 packages in the Arch Linux User Repository (AUR) were compromised to push an infostealer and a stealthy eBPF rootkit, exposing users who installed the tainted packages. Maintainers moved to remove the malicious uploads.

stories.headline: China-linked hackers backdoored Linux login software to hide for nearly a decade
stories.summary: Researchers detailed a China-linked campaign that backdoored Linux login (PAM) software to maintain covert access to targeted systems for nearly ten years, evading detection across long-running intrusions.

stories.headline: phpBB fixes authentication-bypass bug that lurked for a decade
stories.summary: The phpBB project patched an authentication-bypass flaw in its widely used forum software that had gone unnoticed for roughly ten years, leaving boards exposed to account takeover. Admins are urged to update.

stories.headline: US orders Anthropic to suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access for foreign nationals
stories.summary: The US government ordered Anthropic to suspend access to its most advanced AI models — Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 — for foreign nationals inside or outside the US, citing national-security concerns. Anthropic said it would abruptly disable the models for all users while it complies.

stories.headline: Maine disables data-breach notification portal after fake disclosures
stories.summary: Maine took its data-breach notification portal offline after the public submission system was abused to publish fake breach disclosures, prompting a review of how the state validates filings.

stories.headline: Google sues Chinese smishing network accused of using Gemini AI in phishing
stories.summary: Google filed suit against a China-based smishing operation it says abused its Gemini AI to craft phishing messages at scale, part of a broader push against industrialised text-message scams.

stories.headline: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti Sentry flaw within days
stories.summary: CISA added a maximum-severity Ivanti Sentry OS-command-injection flaw (CVE-2026-10520, CVSS 10.0) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog and gave federal agencies until Sunday to patch, citing active exploitation. All Ivanti Sentry users should apply fixes immediately.

stories.headline: ShinyHunters exploit Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day to breach universities
stories.summary: The ShinyHunters group exploited a critical Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day (CVE-2026-35273, CVSS 9.8 — missing authentication for a critical function) to steal data from universities. Oracle issued mitigations and the flaw was added to CISA's KEV catalog; organisations should patch urgently.
stories.source: The Hacker News, BleepingComputer, CISA KEV

stories.headline: Agentjacking attack tricks AI coding agents into running malicious code
stories.summary: Researchers detailed 'Agentjacking,' a class of attacks that manipulate AI coding agents into executing attacker-supplied code, highlighting fresh risk as developers wire autonomous agents into their toolchains.

stories.headline: LangGraph flaw chain exposes self-hosted AI agents to remote code execution
stories.summary: A chain of flaws in LangGraph could let attackers achieve remote code execution against self-hosted AI agents, highlighting growing risk in AI-agent infrastructure. Users are advised to update and lock down deployments.

stories.headline: The Gentlemen ransomware claims 478 victims and can spread like a worm
stories.summary: A ransomware operation calling itself 'The Gentlemen' claims 478 victims and includes worm-like self-propagation, raising the risk of rapid lateral spread inside breached networks.

stories.headline: New 'GreatXML' exploit bypasses Windows BitLocker via recovery-partition XML files
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed a technique dubbed 'GreatXML' that abuses recovery-partition XML files to bypass Windows BitLocker disk encryption, a physical-access risk for unattended or lost devices.

stories.headline: New attacks trick OpenClaw AI agent into running code and leaking secrets
stories.summary: Researchers showed how crafted inputs can trick the OpenClaw AI agent into executing code and leaking secrets, another example of prompt- and tool-abuse risks in autonomous AI assistants.

stories.headline: Pharma giant Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical-trials data
stories.summary: Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk disclosed a security breach involving clinical-trials data. The company said it was investigating the scope and notifying affected parties.

stories.headline: Over 73,000 French government employees affected in Tchap messenger breach
stories.summary: The French government said a breach of its Tchap secure messenger affected more than 73,000 accounts, exposing employee data. Officials said they were investigating and securing the platform.

stories.headline: Japanese energy firm loses drive with data of 10.9 million clients
stories.summary: A Japanese energy company disclosed the loss of a storage drive containing data on about 10.9 million clients, prompting notification and review of its data-handling practices.

stories.headline: INTERPOL takes down Sniper Dz phishing platform, arrests administrator
stories.summary: INTERPOL announced the takedown of the Sniper Dz phishing-as-a-service platform and the arrest of its administrator, disrupting infrastructure used to run large-scale phishing campaigns.

stories.headline: Authorities dismantle 'AudiA6' crypto-laundering service used by ransomware gangs
stories.summary: Europol-coordinated authorities dismantled the 'AudiA6' cryptocurrency-laundering service that ransomware gangs used to cash out, disrupting a key part of the ransomware payment ecosystem.

stories.headline: OceanLotus hits Vietnamese investors with SPECTRALVIPER in 'FireAnt' attack
stories.summary: The OceanLotus (APT32) group targeted Vietnamese investors with the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor in a campaign researchers tracked as 'FireAnt,' continuing the actor's focus on regional espionage.

stories.headline: GitHub to disable npm install scripts by default to curb supply-chain attacks
stories.summary: GitHub said it will disable npm install scripts by default, a hardening change aimed at blunting a common vector for software-supply-chain attacks against Node.js projects.

stories.headline: China-linked JDY botnet expands to 1,500+ devices for cyber reconnaissance
stories.summary: A China-linked botnet tracked as JDY has grown to more than 1,500 compromised devices being used for cyber reconnaissance, researchers said, urging defenders to check exposed devices.

stories.headline: Ivanti, Fortinet and SAP release patches for multiple critical vulnerabilities
stories.summary: Ivanti, Fortinet and SAP shipped fixes for multiple critical vulnerabilities across their products. Administrators should prioritise patching internet-facing and high-value systems.

stories.headline: Langflow vulnerability CVE-2026-5027 exploited for unauthenticated RCE
stories.summary: Attackers are exploiting a Langflow flaw (CVE-2026-5027, CVSS 8.8) to achieve unauthenticated remote code execution on exposed instances of the popular AI-workflow builder. Users should update immediately.

stories.headline: CISA adds Cisco, Chrome and Arista flaws to KEV amid active exploitation
stories.summary: CISA added actively exploited flaws in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (CVE-2026-20245), Google Chrome's V8 engine (CVE-2026-11645, CVSS 8.8) and Arista EOS (CVE-2026-7473) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, setting remediation deadlines for federal agencies.
